gap: tooling — parent-session proof scan hit its 2,048-file discovery cap on a 3,470-session store; execution used an inode-identical hard-link view of the exact current parent JSONL and verified gpt-5.6-sol/ultra
gap: runtime-drift — first post-sync code-architect proof ran gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh rather than inheriting the parent gpt-5.6-sol/ultra; project profiles were current but the loaded plugin cache still carried pre-#687 pins
gap: scheduler — the frozen plan intended exact-file-disjoint n2/n3 writers to co-open, but `open-ready` serialized them after classifying their shared coarse `scripts/` and `plugins/` areas as ambiguous; correctness was preserved but the authored safe frontier did not realize concurrency
gap: scheduler-entrypoint — the frozen plan declared `speculative_open_policy: auto`, and n9-security-review was structurally eligible to overlap the read-only n8-code-review gate, but n8 had already been opened through `open-next`; a subsequent `open-ready` refused with `serial_node_live`, so the attainable DAG shape depended on the lifecycle entrypoint rather than only on the frozen plan and scheduler policy
